content.label: Great app
I’m in the Yosemite valley. I need a charge. This garbage app only makes me “plan a trip” I don’t need to plan Shht! I need a charge!

Effectively always gets who’s in the drive seat wrong. Moves the seat if you get out of the driver’s seat to whomever is in the passenger seat, doesn’t return your seat to you when you get in on the driver’s door. Basically everything that UWB was supposed to solve is worse, not better. 

Also needs the ability to change AC charging amps directly in the app.

title.label: Live activities not working after update

content.label: Good app, but the charging status on live activities needs to refresh more often after the recent update.

the apple watch car key functionality seems to have broken in the latest update. i previously was able to open the apple watch key and use the lock / unlock button, which was useful at home where i have passive entry set to lock only. now when i open the key on apple watch there is no lock/unlock button so i cannot use the watch to unlock my car at home. furthermore it seems like it is not possible to add a complication to the watch face that will simply open the rivian watch app - which would be very useful for quick access to the app on the watch without having to scroll the apps list.

The Rivian app has always been a bit mid. This reached a whole new level of issue since the Rivian “intelligence” rollout. I got a buggy AI assistant I don’t want in exchange for:
- painful 30s-1min wake-ups to unlock the car I am standing next to and just locked 
- even more unreliable multi-factor drive
- constant nags to connect data my car should never need
- a slow and poorly animated “AI” to incorrectly search for Spotify songs

The Rivian is a great car. My only big gripe is that without CarPlay it’s like going back in time ten years on the media experience. 

They’ve built their own versions of Maps, Spotify, etc, and none of them are very good. AND they charge you $15/month to use them.

My only other big gripe with the car is the doors don’t open when you walk up to it. You have to touch your phone to the door.

I downloaded the latest app update as well as the vehicle 2026.15.0 update within the last 48 hours. By coincidence I also bought a new smart phone. I now am able to access and drive the vehicle ONLY with a physical key card. I cannot add or delete a digital key through either the app or the Apple wallet. It seems Rivian failed to do all the quality control work on the back end to make sure the little things still worked before rolling out major software updates. What a crock.
App shows car not charging on phone Home Screen. App shows car unplugged in app energy.

The app is basically useless on Gen1 vehicles, which is an obvious sign of how Rivian will run in the future with forced obsolescence. Proximity is buggy and aggressive - sometimes it responds to proximity, other times it doesn’t. If you have proximity on, you CANNOT use any of the remote functions when you’re not close to the vehicle. If you have proximity turned off, the remote app is hit or miss. I have prompted for a frunk open, and the app sent the request 10 minutes later.
This recent update broke my home screen widget which was the primary way I had used the app. Now I just see a black box.
Latest update broke public charging.  Nothing happens after selecting the charger.  App is completely useless.  Actually do QA before you release and break public charging at Rivian chargers!

title.label: Why is it so hard to charge with low level chargers?

content.label:
It says “plug in to charge” ok, plug in, won’t charge. Says “select charger to start charging” ok, I select the charger no option to start just says “charger available” 

Can’t you just be like charge point and just let us tap and plug in? Why these extra steps that most of the time DON’T WORK? Location: Las Vegas Town Square

This app/charger for low level is almost always terrible but now it’s just unusable.
The latest version no longer updates on the iPhone lock screen. It shows time to charge and state of charge but never updates.
Just when you thought Tesla had the worst customer service on earth, Rivian now takes the prize.

title.label: Latest update brings back annoying bug

content.label:
For several months, the Rivian app finally removed the notification every single time you closed the app “to use your phone as a key, you’ll need the app open”

Well, now that notification is back, every SINGLE time you close the app. 

I know for a fact you don’t need the app open to use your phone as a key! Please fix this Rivian it’s SO annoying.

Barcode on the charging station cannot be scanned, I need to search for the station on the map, and couldn’t find mine. They place them intentionally near places with bad signal, Yosemite for example, where the whole experience relies on having solid internet connection. Bad user experience
AC Transit in Bay Area CA allows employees access to Tesla accounts app on their work phones but restricts employees to access Rivian accounts. What’s up with that?

As the title says, I haven’t taken delivery yet, but so far I’m a little disappointed with the app coming from a Tesla. With the Tesla app, I could see how much I charged at home over the last 30 days and use that amount to pay toward the electric bill. The Rivian app doesn’t seem to have that option.

Edit: The app should have a way to filter compatible charging stations by my vehicle and adapters I have.
Sometimes it lets me into the car. Other times I get a ride with friends and that’s cool too.

title.label: Genuinely shocked at how bad the “phone key” is

content.label:
So there are two scenarios that occur when I'm using this app and my R1T (brand new by the way):
1. Often my car unlocks and locks back and forth when my phone happens to be near my car but I don't even want to get into it. This could be when I'm at home or I'm standing near it or sitting near it at a coffee shop. It just opens and it unlocks and locks.
2. When I do want to get in my car, often I walk up to my car, my phone is in my pocket, and I stand right next to the car. It won't recognize it. It'll just keep on staying locked so then I have to open the app and hit unlock or use the NFC.


Good Lord Rivian, please work on phone key performance. Like Tesla is absolutely night and day better and more reliable than your system. 

Also it feels like I can't run commands over Bluetooth from the app. It seems like I have to use the network, which is crazy because I'm right next to it. Again Tesla has had the ability to send all commands over BLE with no network for years now. Why does a modern brand new car company like Rivian, shipping amazing products, have these issues?

title.label: Unable to log into app after update

content.label: I have 2 Rivians and after taking the latest update for my quad R1T 2023 the mobile app said update at 0% in progress even though I started the update from the vehicle cable screen.  I closed the app, then logged out of the app, tried to log back in (and even getting the verification code).  After thinking about signing me in and just keeps looping back to the sign in screen.  I even uninstalled the app, rebooted my phone, and the same re-login loop/cycle is still occurring.  At the moment I have no access to either on of my vehicle from my phone app.

title.label: Digital key doesn’t work on Apple Watch

content.label: The process of getting digital key on Apple Watch fails. Rivian support redirects to Apple. Apple redirects to Rivian. Customers suffer.

The app is very buggy, I tried to submit a service request, it is horrible and fail every time. Rivian, do you have a proper software test and release process in place? I had much more respect for the company before I buy a car from them. Less than 4 months after receiving my RIT, one of the cameras stopped working. Now I not able to request a service appointment through app, it keeps failing. And don’t  get me to start on the so-called AI assistant that they added to the app, its main task is to drive the users crazy and stop them from accessing to human.

Bugs everywhere. App has issues where features don’t work frequently. Digital keys also randomly don’t work. This is a pretty beta level car and the app is the same. Wanted to like the brand but many things just don’t work.

V3.9 has great new features (love the Apple Watch app) but it seems as though it’s introduced a few bugs, the most annoying one is the fact that it doesn’t wake up right away when you bring it to the foreground so things like charging and percent charged take a minute or so to show up. This never happened before as far as I remember.

According to my app the Rivian is always at home, even though the app shows it is driving. The charging schedule shows in the app when at other locations, but it doesn’t actually go by the schedule, the schedule is not supposed to show. And there are just so many more issues. It is just about only good to lock and unlock the vehicle.
And why is the app all the sudden rated for 18years plus? In the US teens can start driving at 15. But all the sudden with the latest update my teenage daughter needed approval by me to use the app. Seems ridiculous.
Great app. Simple. Easy to use. I can tell a lot of effort went into the UI. I can’t think of anything to improve, but I love all the meaningful updates they provide. Keep up the great work Rivian!

One must select charger based upon that charger’s unique code to begin charging. This should correspond to the code listed in the app. The codes in the app are completely different from those on the charge stations. That’s fun. No way to use the chargers. Another dead EV charging service. Just what we needed. Will never trust Rivian.

Every time I preheat my car, the vehicle reaches certain temperature below what I had it set for, then it compete disables all remote climate control, I can’t turn it off or on, can’t set temperature! This is so frustrating when it’s -20 F in the morning and can’t get this to work properly. Also, please have a separate way to turn on the wiper heaters! 
App also takes forever to connect to the car.

Seems that with this latest update, the widgets (both lock screen and Home Screen) wake the car up every 30-ish minutes to check status (my guess, but the car will not actually go to “sleep”). This causes the main battery to drop by 5-10% per 24hr period. This is on a 2026 R1S Dual Large Pack.

title.label: Bug. Cannot share a digital key. iPhone

content.label: Cannot share a digital key via airdrop and message. No content included in message. When airdrop, it shows no recipient.

title.label: A frustration for me, and an embarrassment for Rivian

content.label:
I’m up in Yosemite, hoping to charge my Tesla with the Rivian chargers here. Downloaded the app, but it seemed all about buying a Rivian, not how to use Rivian chargers.

Called Rivian 800-support. They were absolutely useless. The person told me the Rivian chargers were out of service, and that is why I couldn’t charge. That turned out to be 100% false.

I finally found the only staff person at The Ahwahnee who could walk me through the arcane path to connect with a Rivian charger here.

I am now charging fine, but absent this one knowledgeable staff person, I would be dead-in-the-water…and with dead Tesla EV.
App launch takes more than 1 minute (perhaps when cache exists). Need to restart my phone to launch it.

I loved the new apple key card integration on my iPhone 13. It worked so well. I could keep my phone in my pocket FINALLY and didn’t have to tie on the screen for it to work. I upgraded to an iPhone 17 Pro over the holidays. I constantly have to take my phone out of my bag or pocket and turn on the screen for it to work

This update to the Rivian app brings native UWB Car Key support to not just the iPhone but also the Apple Watch. Also I can share a key with others electronically, so for example, extended family members can return to the vehicle and chill while the rest of the family can continue enjoying the theme park, etc. & come and go as they please.

title.label: App doesn’t work for charger waypoints

content.label: Tried to charge and it didn’t work. The charger says to find xcp 4 in the app but the app doesn’t have that. Couldn’t charge. Super disappointing as the app was otherwise sleek
Frustrating in many way. Biggest issue is my car doesn’t show proper location in my app.

Y’all, I swear I’m not a complete dummy, I work on apps for a living, but I only just realized I can tap on the stall listed for a given Adventure Network location to start the charge on my Ioniq 5.

There is no microcopy or affordance on the screen with the list of chargers indicating that you have to *tap* the chargers in the list to get the “start charge” button, and the items in the list don’t *look* interactive in the least.

All it says is “Tap to pay or start charging in the app,” but not *how* to start charging. So I’ve been tapping my card to pay the last few times I used the Rivian network.

This is the one thing Electrify America does better, their UI is clear and has instructions.

Tap to pay is fine, but I want the detailed receipt and live status.

Thanks y’all, and if you fix this I promise I’ll buy an R3 to replace my Ioniq 5.
We’re still waiting for the latest software updates

title.label: No longer works unless you are next to the car

content.label: Keep getting worst. The car shows full cellular bars, but the app keeps saying car in not connected to the cloud

title.label: Software upgrade locked out of digital key.

content.label: 2025.46 is terrible. Got a software upgrade overnight. Now my husband and I are both no longer have a key on our phones. It says to be in the car to update it, on the WiFi, which we were, and nothing. It won’t connect. Have to use the physical key. It removed the existing digital key (which worked just fine) to replace it with a new one in the apple wallet, but what good is it if the key doesn’t actually download? Just keep getting an error.

title.label: App does not work with car

content.label: The app is downright not functioning whatsoever. The vehicle is stuck in sleep mode and cannot wake it up from the app. Cannot do any function whatever as the car remains in sleep mode. Had even called Rivian support and they were unable to help and offered to tow the vehicle. Paying $1k/month for a vehicle you cannot use is ridiculous. Love the vehicle when it is working. Will update the review when I have other news to share.

Very poorly made. Just updated it and now my widget is all black box. Nothing on it. This always happens , by time vehicle or app is updated something that is working previous stops working.

Now it’s asked me to set up key again after deleting it even though it shows my key in the car. 

Rivian found their software team on Temu. Pathetic, Tesla has been doing this for a decade with very few issues. 

App takes anywhere from 45 seconds to 3 minutes to connect to just unlock the car. 

The client experience has been a dumpster fire. 

Should have just bought the Model X plus would have gotten the EV credit. 

Worthless app and car software.

title.label: Has the functionality but lacks stability

content.label: Call Miss comparing to Tesla. But this app has a lot of issues several times. I can’t even connect to the car even though I can clearly see the car that is connected to Wi-Fi. Also, I’m not showing properly to charge speeds when I want to see it.
Need to be able to set battery preconditioning from the app. Pls add that the app since it takes a while for battery to precondition.

This is as much a review of the Rivian system as the app.

Hate that my phone calls get hijacked by my car when I get near the vehicle while talking on the phone. 

Should get a prompt asking if I want to transfer call to vehicle.
Never stays online with connection. App is glitching all the time. Can’t update anything

Used to be one of the better apps. In the more recent updates, they constantly prompt you to enable full time location services even in the background when not using the app and to charge on what they deem is the best“eco” timing during the day regardless of your energy setup, location and situation.

WTH? Makes me not want to use this vehicle anymore. If they continue this trajectory, I will not be getting another Rivian.

They have fallen way behind on basic features like maps, apps and automated driving amongst other areas. Usability is all they have left and they’re blowing it with heavy handed, very irritating forced behaviors. Just stop Rivian.

1. Can’t set multiple locations for scheduled charging (ie for multiple houses)

2. Charging schedule intermittently doesn’t work (wake up to uncharged vehicle)

3. Windows don’t close when I walk away / lock
